commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=34abca290ce1aabd4eedaf39d0ab26eaba7696b4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR converts sandbox registry, sandbox image, and sandbox-scope doctor diagnostics into structured health checks with dry-run/effects/note handling and related tests.

Reproducibility: yes. From the PR head source, `repairSandboxImages` cannot distinguish declined or failed image builds but still emits a change line, and the repair runner then treats the omitted status as repaired before detect-after reports leftovers.
commands.review_followup: Human/contributor follow-up is needed because the PR is draft/protected/stacked, real behavior proof is missing, and the remaining merge decision depends on updated proof after code fixes.

target.title: Doctor: structure sandbox repair checks
target.body:
## Goal

Convert the sandbox repair bucket to the structured doctor contract while preserving sandbox ownership boundaries.

This PR makes sandbox registry, image, and scope diagnostics first-class health checks. The main goal is to make sandbox maintenance auditable without accidentally turning operator-owned Docker images or read-only scope warnings into automatic repairs.

## Scope

| Check | Goal | Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files` | Represent legacy sandbox registry migration as structured state repair. | Detect finds legacy registry files; repair runs the existing sharded migration path. |
| `core/doctor/sandbox/images` | Separate repairable default sandbox images from operator-managed custom images. | Detect reports missing/stale/default/custom/Docker-unavailable cases; repair only handles repairable default-image work. |
| `core/doctor/sandbox-scope` | Keep scope warnings visible but read-only. | Detect emits findings; no automatic mutation is offered. |

## Structured Repair Matrix

| Check | Detect / lint behavior | Repair behavior | Dry-run / effects | Detect-after / note proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files` | Detects legacy sandbox registry files read-only. | Runs the existing sharded registry migration through the structured repair path. | State effects describe container/process registry migration; no fake single-file diff. | Detect-after confirms legacy monolithic registry paths are no longer reported; migration tests assert files/locks are gone and sharded entries are readable. |
| `core/doctor/sandbox/images` | Detects missing/stale sandbox images, Docker-unavailable cases, and operator-managed custom images without printing. | Converted repair calls `repairSandboxImages(...)`; legacy `maybeRepairSandboxImages(...)` remains the old print wrapper. | Process effects distinguish buildable default image repairs from custom/operator-managed image skips. | Repair guidance uses shared `DoctorPrompter.note(...)`, routed by structured adapters into ordered repair notes. |
| `core/doctor/sandbox-scope` | Emits read-only scope findings. | No automatic mutation. | No repair effects or diffs because no repair is available. | Stays lintable/read-only and does not participate in repaired validation. |
| Custom image boundary | Custom base/browser images remain operator-managed findings/warnings. | Structured repair skips them with actionable guidance rather than invoking default build scripts. | Skip/operator effects stay explicit. | Focused tests lock custom image behavior and Docker-unavailable warnings. |

## Proof

| Proof | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Focused sandbox/core/repair/lint tests | 4 focused files / 46 tests passed on current head. |
| Earlier broader focused proof | 5 files / 66 tests passed locally and in Crabbox run `run_1807262a6457`. |
| Formatting and whitespace checks | Passed. |
| Typecheck and autoreview from recorded proof | Passed. |

## Out of Scope

- Building or pulling real Docker images during this PR’s proof.
- Treating custom sandbox images as automatically repairable.
- Changing sandbox runtime execution behavior.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Structured doctor repair conversion for sandbox registry files, sandbox image checks, and sandbox scope warnings with isomorphic `detect -> repair/dry-run -> detect` semantics and operator-managed custom image handling.

Real environment tested: Local WSL source checkout plus direct AWS Crabbox lease `cbx_c86ebac61277` (`tidal-hermit`), run `run_1807262a6457`, OpenClaw source checkout synced from this branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused sandbox doctor tests, formatting, diff check, tsgo, autoreview, and Crabbox focused test retry.

Evidence after fix: Local format, diff, tsgo, focused tests, and autoreview passed. Crabbox run `run_1807262a6457` exited 0 with 5 test files / 66 tests passed. Proof artifact: https://gist.github.com/giodl73-repo/c6ff5e2d3d3d5ca04e5fd3c5fb0c660d.

Observed result after fix: structured dry-run reports sandbox registry migration effects without mutation; sandbox image dry-run reports repairable default image setup separately from operator-managed custom images; repair skips non-repairable custom images with actionable warnings; sandbox scope findings stay read-only.

What was not tested: full changed gate, actual Docker image build/pull, package/update survivor lanes, and live Docker sandbox execution.
target.author: giodl73-repo
